h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically apply the food-to-eater rule to correct the remaining arrows on the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent instruction strategy throughout the dialogue without any major reversal or adaptation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent instruction strategy throughout the dialogue without any major reversal or adaptation.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent instruction strategy throughout the dialogue without any major reversal or adaptation.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to repeat the distinction between side and perimeter scales while complaining about the identical numbers.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
New gate: each answer needs a receipt sentence before it can stand.

Write the measured object first:

Side scale: ______, ___ to ___, factor ___.
Perimeter scale: ______, ___ to ___, factor ___.
Area scale: ______, ___ to ___, factor ___.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the task to requiring 'receipt sentences' to address the learner's persistent complaint that the bare numbers look identical and are not enough.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: so I think that is the test rule
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to rely on the physical grid to count squares for larger factors.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: No boxes now. Old area: s^2. New side: ks. New area: (ks)^2. Write the next equality.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from concrete grid-based counting to abstract algebraic representation to force a general rule.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn occurred, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn occurred, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.
